item.title: EMS was called to Mitch McConnell's home for "unconscious" patient last month
item.description: On the same morning Sen. Mitch McConnell was hospitalized last month, EMS personnel went to his home to respond to an unconscious person who appeared to experience "cardiac arrest," according to a dispatch call.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Fraunces Tavern: Where Americans can party like a founding father
item.description: If you want to party like a founding father, Fraunces Tavern in New York City promises that experience this July 4. The owner says the Sons of Liberty drank, argued and celebrated there. Tony Dokoupil has the story.

item.title: Trump made over $1 billion on crypto ventures last year, disclosure shows
item.description: President Trump earned more than a billion dollars from crypto-related ventures alone last year, according to a financial disclosure, including from his meme coin business and his family's cryptocurrency firm.

item.title: NCAA chief: No plan to change rules on transgender athletes after SCOTUS ruling
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told CBS News he doesn't think the group will need to change its rules on transgender athletes in light of a Supreme Court ruling that allowed states to ban their participation.

item.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

item.title: Trump takes first trip on Qatari-gifted Air Force One
item.description: President Trump is traveling Wednesday to North Dakota in what marks his first trip aboard the new Air Force One, which was gifted to the U.S. by the Qatari government last year.

item.title: This July 4th could be hottest ever in parts of U.S.
item.description: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

item.title: More trucks unload outside of Madison Square Garden ahead of speculated Swift-Kelce wedding
item.description: Fan fever is rising over the Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding as forklifts, bins and boxes arrive outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City. Jo Ling Kent has more details.

item.title: Trump acknowledges he made $1.4 billion in 2025 crypto ventures, raising ethical questions
item.description: After the Office of Government Ethics revealed just how much crypto money the president has been making, Tony Dokoupil asks how this sort of thing is allowed ethically and legally.

item.title: Roads and airports packed with millions of July 4 travelers
item.description: Passengers aboard American Airlines Flight 1040 had a scary start to the July 4 travel rush after the pilot announced there was a reported engine fire. Though American says there was no evidence of that. Kris Van Cleave has more on the holiday rush.

item.title: Empire State Building climbers engaged and under arrest
item.description: Two people in black masks scaled the Empire State Building, scrambling the New York City Police Department. They call themselves "roof-toppers," or stunt climbers, and they unfurled a banner about world peace before one of them got out a ring and proposed. By the time they got down, they were engaged and under arrest. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Jaylen Brown reportedly traded from Celtics to 76ers
item.description: Jaylen Brown is done with the Boston Celtics. The Celtics have traded Brown to Philadelphia 76ers.

item.title: England comes back with 2 goals from Harry Kane in World Cup
item.description: England advanced in the World Cup on Wednesday with a late goal from its all-time leading scorer, Harry Kane. Meanwhile, the U.S. Men's National Team is in a win-or-go-home match in the San Francisco Bay Area. Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: Game show icons Kitty Carlisle's and Bert Convy's 1976 "Bicentennial Minutes" segments
item.description: CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett introduces two 1976 CBS segments marking America's bicentennial, featuring game show regulars Kitty Carlisle and Bert Convy.

item.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
item.description: Here's a look at how events have unfolded in the Karen Read case.

item.title: Historian Jon Meacham on the national mood surrounding America 250
item.description: Historian Jon Meacham is serving as the National Constitution Center's semiquincentennial scholar for the nation's 250th birthday. Meacham joins "The Takeout" to discuss the festivities and how Americans are feeling about the anniversary.

item.title: Kamala Harris reached out to Mamdani and pro-Palestinian activists, Axios reports
item.description: Axios reports that former Vice President Kamala Harris has reached out to New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani and pro-Palestinian activists. Political strategists Cassie Smedile and Hyma Moore join with analysis.

item.title: Fans fired up as U.S. prepares to take on Bosnia in World Cup knockout stage
item.description: World Cup fever is in full swing in California, where the U.S. Men's National Team is preparing to take the field on Wednesday in its first knockout stage match of the tournament against Bosnia and Herzegovina. CBS News correspondent Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: 2 Empire State Building climbers in custody after apparently getting engaged at the top
item.description: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.

item.title: Who climbed the Empire State Building? Here's what we know.
item.description: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angelina Nikolau, 33.

item.title: What democratic socialist wins mean for Democratic Party's future
item.description: Democratic socialists celebrated yet another primary victory in Colorado on Wednesday. Newcomer Melat Kiros ended Congresswoman Diana DeGette's 30-year hold on the state's first congressional district. CBS News' Anthony Salvanto breaks it down.

item.title: Feds investigating social adult daycares paid for with tax dollars
item.description: Dozens of Medicaid-funded social adult daycare centers are packed into one New York City neighborhood, a CBS News data analysis has found. And national investigative correspondent Tom Hanson learned federal authorities are looking into possible fraudulent activity at some facilities across New York state.

item.title: CBS News journalists unpack rumored Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding at Madison Square Garden
item.description: Rumors are swirling about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce tying the knot at Madison Square Garden. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Gabrielle Ake, self-described Swifties, join "The Takeout" with the latest.

item.title: Trump claims he isn't involved in his personal finances after crypto disclosure
item.description: President Trump claimed Wednesday he is not involved in his personal financial dealings. Disclosure forms released Tuesday revealed he made more than a billion dollars in cryptocurrency ventures last year. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: Sweeping changes to federal student loans take effect
item.description: Significant changes to federal student loan policies took effect on Wednesday. CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger joins "The Takeout" to explain what borrowers need to know.

item.title: One NYC neighborhood has dozens of adult daycares. Now the feds have questions.
item.description: Investigations are underway into possible fraudulent activity at some of these kinds of facilities across New York, CBS News has learned.

item.title: 7/1: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump filing reveals he made $2.2 billion in 2025; H. R. McMaster says Iran is "getting everything that they wanted" in U.S. talks.

item.title: U.S. says it won't extend key trade deal with Canada and Mexico
item.description: The United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement will remain in effect until it expires in 2036, unless the countries strike another deal to extend it.

item.title: CBS to host "The Great American Block Party 250" on July 4
item.description: "CBS Evening News" anchor Tony Dokoupil and "Entertainment Tonight" co-host Nischelle Turner will host "The Great American Block Party 250," a primetime special on July 4 celebrating America's 250th birthday from Washington, D.C.
